problem: Live DJs need to compose performance-aware prompts (genre, transition, crowd intent, set objective, FX, performance goal, venue, era) and get an AI-expanded recommendation without leaving the booth or relying on a server/API key. Existing prompt tools are generic text boxes that ignore DJ context (crowd heat, risk, drop fatigue) and don't translate selections into actionable live-set guidance.
stakeholders: Primary: working DJs and producers (personas include DJ Jazzy Jeff — workflow/reliability lens; Brian Eno — generative/process lens; Alessandro Cortini and Kaitlyn Aurelia Smith — instrument-character lens; Trent Reznor, Max Cooper, REZZ — performance/production lenses). Secondary: solo maintainer (Geoff Seemueller), GitHub Pages visitors, the Caretta autopilot agent acting on the repo.
evidence: README.md describes 'AR DJ copilot interface' with 8-axis composition, telemetry (objective, transitionIntent, crowdHeat, risk, dropFatigue, recommendation) and an example prompt tail. src/domain/categories.ts encodes the 8 DJ-flavored axes. src/application/djCockpit.ts owns DJ_CATEGORY_LABELS; contexts/dj/state.ts is a MobX-State-Tree DJ machine prototype with injected audio/analyzer/ai services. .caretta/personas/*.json define seven domain personas used to evaluate the tool. CLAUDE.md confirms layered Clean Architecture and deployment to GitHub Pages.
desired_outcome: A static, zero-backend AR DJ copilot where selecting dimensions on a 3D synth-style control surface yields (a) a performance-aware prompt, (b) live telemetry that drives HUD/visuals, and (c) an LLM-expanded foundation — all running locally via WebGPU, persisted across reloads, and deployable as a static site to GitHub Pages.
constraints: Static site only (no server, no API key); base path '/cortex-enigma/'; LLM inference requires WebGPU (Chrome/Edge 113+, excludes Firefox/Safari by default); React 19 + TS 6 + Vite 8 + Three.js stack; layered Clean Architecture (presentation→application→domain, infrastructure implements ports); no separate test files historically (now vitest+playwright added); styled-components must be SSR-bundled (noExternal) for prerender.
dependencies: @mlc-ai/web-llm (Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct via WebGPU), @react-three/fiber + drei + postprocessing, three.js 0.183, styled-components 6, React 19, Vite 8, lucide-react, maath. Dev: vitest 4 + Playwright (browser tests), TypeScript 6, ESLint 9. CI: bun + Playwright chromium. Deploy: GitHub Actions → GitHub Pages. Optional: mobx-state-tree for the experimental DJ machine in contexts/dj.
existing_systems: Cortex Enigma itself (src/) — useSelections + SelectionService persist to localStorage; usePromptEngine wraps WebLLMAdapter; CortexCanvas renders the 3D synth scene with knobs/HUD/effects; prerender.mjs SSR-pre-renders for /dist. Experimental contexts/dj MST machine models decks, track analysis, crowd state, transitions, rain mode. Caretta autopilot GitHub Action operates on the repo on schedule/events. references/ contains art-compatibility matrices likely fed into prompt logic.
hypotheses: 1) Translating raw selections into DJ telemetry (heat/risk/fatigue) before LLM expansion produces more useful prompts than raw category strings. 2) A tactile 3D synth surface increases creative engagement vs. checkbox UIs — but only if it stays performant on mid-range laptops. 3) In-browser WebGPU LLM is fast enough for live use; users will accept the WebGPU browser restriction. 4) Persona-driven evaluation (DJ Jazzy Jeff workflow vs Eno generative lens) will surface different blocking issues than generic usability testing.
success_metrics: Successful SSR pre-render and Pages deploy on every push to master (CI green). All vitest suites pass (SelectionService, buildDJRichContext, categories, promptBuilder). LLM expansion completes locally without errors. Selection state survives reload. Telemetry-driven HUD/core glow responds to selection changes. Persona reviews (per .caretta/personas) flag no rejection_no_if triggers.
tradeoffs: WebGPU-only LLM = no server cost but excludes Safari/Firefox users. Static GitHub Pages = simple deploy but no analytics/personalization. Layered Clean Architecture adds indirection (ports/adapters) for a small app, but isolates LLM and storage so they can be swapped. Heavy 3D scene + bloom/noise/vignette = visually distinctive but raises GPU baseline and complicates accessibility. Keeping the DJ MST machine in contexts/dj/ unwired preserves optionality but risks dead code.
risks: WebGPU compatibility excludes a large share of users. Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct quality may be too weak for nuanced DJ-context expansion. 3D scene + LLM together can OOM mid-range devices. Telemetry heuristics in djCockpit.ts may feel arbitrary to working DJs (DJ Jazzy Jeff persona risk). Drift between the active djCockpit telemetry and the unused buildDJRichContext formatter / MST machine creates two sources of truth. Static hosting limits ability to ship hotfixes for runtime LLM issues. Caretta autopilot has write access to the repo — autonomous changes could regress prompt quality.
assumption_summary: We assume DJs want a prompt-composition tool (not a deck-control tool yet), that WebGPU is acceptable as a hard requirement, that the 8 chosen axes cover the meaningful DJ-prompt dimensions, that local Llama-3.2-1B produces useful expansion, and that a 3D synth metaphor is more engaging than a form UI for this audience.
frame_notes: Project frames itself as an 'AR DJ copilot' but currently ships a prompt composer with telemetry-driven visuals — not a deck controller. Two parallel context models exist (djCockpit live, buildDJRichContext + MST machine experimental); decide which is canonical before expanding scope. The Caretta autopilot persona-driven evaluation system is a distinctive framing layer worth preserving as a design discipline.
risk_notes: No explicit risk acceptance recorded in repo; CLAUDE.md and README acknowledge WebGPU restriction as a known limitation, not a formally accepted risk.

assumptions.evidence: README claims WebGPU works in Chrome/Edge 113+; no telemetry on actual visitor browser share
assumptions.validation_next_step: Add anonymous WebGPU-capability check on page load and log to a static counter or GH Pages-compatible analytic

assumptions.evidence: Llama-3.2-1B is the smallest practical model; no recorded eval of expansion quality on DJ prompts
assumptions.validation_next_step: Score 20 representative selection sets through the LLM and rate against persona criteria (DJ Jazzy Jeff workflow, Eno generative)

assumptions.evidence: Categories.ts has 8 axes; buildDJRichContext + djCockpit derive telemetry; tests exist for promptBuilder and buildDJRichContext
assumptions.validation_next_step: Run persona reviews on sample prompts to confirm axis coverage

assumptions.evidence: 3D scene with bloom/noise/vignette is visually rich but no perf budget recorded
assumptions.validation_next_step: Lighthouse + WebGPU+three.js perf profile on mid-range laptop and mobile-class GPU

frame_comparisons.framing: Help DJs compose performance-aware AI prompts with telemetry-driven visuals
frame_comparisons.tradeoffs: Ships now, narrow scope; not a real performance tool
frame_comparisons.recommendation: Keep as v1; explicitly label as composition aid

frame_comparisons.framing: Real-time assistant during a set: deck state, crowd reading, transition recommendation
frame_comparisons.evidence: contexts/dj/state.ts MST machine models decks/crowd/transition lifecycle but is unwired
frame_comparisons.tradeoffs: Matches README marketing; requires audio I/O, latency budget, browser audio APIs
frame_comparisons.recommendation: Treat as v2; do not block v1 deploy

frame_comparisons.framing: A small system whose rules and constraints produce surprising outputs over long sessions
frame_comparisons.evidence: Persona definition .caretta/personas/persona-brian-eno.json; 8-axis CATEGORIES as rule set
frame_comparisons.tradeoffs: Drops DJ-specific framing; broadens appeal but loses sharp positioning
frame_comparisons.recommendation: Hold; revisit if DJ framing fails persona review

decision_log.rationale: No backend cost, simple CI; aligns with zero-API-key in-browser LLM choice
decision_log.rejected_alternatives: Server-rendered Next.js app with hosted LLM; Cloudflare Workers AI
decision_log.reversibility: Medium — can move to Workers/Pages Functions later without rewriting domain/application layers

decision_log.rationale: Removes API key, server cost, and per-user latency to a hosted model
decision_log.rejected_alternatives: Hosted Claude/OpenAI API; larger local model

decision_log.rationale: Isolates domain logic from React/Three/WebLLM so adapters can be swapped
decision_log.rejected_alternatives: Flat React app with inline logic
decision_log.reversibility: Low — refactoring back would lose port boundaries

risk_dashboard.mitigation: Detect WebGPU, show non-LLM mode (selections + prompt only) with clear browser guidance

risk_dashboard.trigger: LLM output quality fails persona review
risk_dashboard.mitigation: Tune system prompt; consider larger quantized model or fallback hosted endpoint behind feature flag

risk_dashboard.trigger: 3D scene + LLM exceed GPU/RAM on mid-tier device
risk_dashboard.mitigation: Reduce postprocessing passes; lazy-load LLM only on user action; add a 'lite' visual mode
Pick one canonical formatter; delete or clearly mark the other as experimental

risk_dashboard.trigger: Caretta autopilot ships regression to master
risk_dashboard.mitigation: Require CI green + persona-review gate before merge; keep deploy from master not directly from autopilot branches

dependency_graph.reason: Hooks (useSelections, usePromptEngine) call SelectionService and djCockpit
SelectionService and telemetry helpers operate on CATEGORIES, types, and buildPrompt
LocalStorageAdapter and WebLLMAdapter implement IStoragePort and ILLMPort
Hooks wire concrete adapters into the React tree
npm run build runs tsc then SSR-prerenders entry-server.tsx to dist/
GitHub Pages workflow runs bun run build and uploads dist/
CI runs browser tests via Playwright chromium
State machine depends on environment-provided services to stay deterministic
GitHub Action with write permissions acts on repo events on schedule
